personal.name: Vansh Virani
personal.title: Software Engineer
personal.summary: Software Engineer and incoming Data Science graduate student at SJSU with expertise in architecting scalable backend services and cloud-native ML pipelines. Proficient in building RAG systems, distributed model monitoring, and full-stack applications. Demonstrated success deploying production-grade solutions within Agile environments.

projects.title: GoldenPath
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack Tutoring Platform
projects.description: Collaborated in a cross-functional Agile team of 7 to design, build, and deploy a tutoring platform serving 30,000+ users — contributing across the full software development lifecycle.
bullets: Designed a scalable relational DB schema with Django ORM and denormalization, enabling sub-second API response times
Deployed on AWS EC2 with Apache for high-availability infrastructure supporting concurrent user sessions
Implemented secure RESTful APIs with campus-domain email auth and password hashing
Integrated Splunk logging and Google Analytics for observability and data-driven debugging

projects.title: Savaalish
projects.subtitle: Generative AI Simulation Pipeline
projects.description: Cloud-native data pipeline evaluating generative AI model behaviour through automated knowledge-task simulations with a production-ready RAG engine.
bullets: Engineered token-aware chunking and FAISS vector retrieval for production-ready RAG pipeline
Built RESTful APIs for reproducible experimentation following microservices design patterns
Architected modular pipeline orchestration for automated knowledge-task simulations

projects.title: Skin Cancer Classifier
projects.subtitle: Cloud-Native ML Pipeline
projects.description: CNN-based dermatoscopic image classifier deployed on Kubernetes with full MLOps observability — ResNet and EfficientNet with transfer learning on imbalanced datasets.
bullets: Containerised and orchestrated training/inference workloads on Kubernetes for scalable distributed experiments
Applied transfer learning and cross-validation for model generalisation on imbalanced datasets
Integrated Prometheus + Grafana for GPU utilisation and model drift monitoring

projects.title: Gator Goods
projects.subtitle: Campus Marketplace SPA
projects.description: End-to-end e-commerce single-page application for SFSU students to list and purchase items with real-time order tracking and optimised SQL performance.
bullets: Optimised SQL query performance by 35% through indexed schemas and debounced search filters
Built RESTful APIs enabling listing management, order processing, and real-time status tracking
Led version control and code review practices, enforcing branching strategy across the team
